title: Product Reqs Doc
body:
**Macplants ERP — Full Requirements Overview.**

**Purpose:** Complete list of every feature we're building for Stage 1 of MacPlants ERP build, written for team discussion.

title: Data Model
body: **Convention:** every Data Model block in this document lists the core persisted fields for each object — i.e. the columns that would actually live in the database. Derived values (lifetime value, current balance, quote/order/invoice totals, line totals, reserved-quantity rollups, etc.) are computed on demand from the underlying records and are NOT stored as fields. They show up in the UI sections of each PRD instead.

title: User
body:
| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| role | enum | Super Admin / Admin |
| name | string |  |
| email | string |  |
| active | boolean |  |
| created\_at | datetime |  |
| last\_login\_at | datetime | nullable |

title: \- Login Permissions
body:
The ERP and customer portals support four login roles. **Staff members do not have a login** — they use the PWA only for stock updates and cannot access the ERP or any customer portal.

* **Super Admin** — Full ERP access plus the ability to add/remove staff PWA accounts and view the user-action audit log (see PRD-5.0). Super Admin \= Josh only.

* **Admin** — Full ERP access. Create POs, quotes, and orders; update stock; manage customers. Admin \= Jake and office staff.

* **Trade Customer** — Logs into the trade portal to search plants, request quotes, and place orders against account credit (see PRD-2.1.1).

* **Retail Customer** — Logs into the retail portal to browse retail-ready stock and place mail-order purchases (see PRD-2.1.2).

* **Staff Member (no login)** — Nursery team. Uses…

title: What Is It
body: The foundation of the entire system. A single database that holds every plant variety Macplants grows or sells, every pot size, every price, and every batch of stock — whether it's available, growing, on order from a supplier, or reserved for a customer. This replaces GrowMaster as the source of truth for stock.

title: Plant Settings
body:
* In Settings \> Plants. List all of the plants in the database. This will detail Plant Name, Plant Sizes, Available on WebShop Boolean, VAT Applicable boolean, Special Plant Boolean.

* In Settings \> Plant Settings. The user can see all plants that have the boolean 'Special Plant: True'. The User can add or remove any plant from this list easily. This boolean flag applies to all of the POT Sizes of that plant.

* In Settings \> Plant Settings. Use can set the VAT as 20%, 0% or NO VAT, this is a dropdown on each plant & will apply to all batches of that plant

* In Settings Plants \> Add Plant — Admin can Create a New Plant which is added to the database, this will automatically generate the SKU. User will be prompted to full out Plant Name, description etc & all prices of different plant…

title: Plant List
body:
* User can click on plant, view all different pot sizes, cost price, trade price and retail price. This works the same as on the plant table.

* User will be able to toggle each pot size as ON or OFF for the Retail Webshop available. User will see the plant category as a pill.

* Edit icon on the right hand side. User clicks on this and then they can see the plant name, description, care instructions, can edit prices & dimensions (text field) for each pot size for cost, trade or retail.

* Each Plant supports multiple pot sizes per plant (7cm, 9cm, 1L, 2L, 2LD, 3L), each with its own SKU, retail price, trade price, cost price, and dimensions.

title: Plant Batches
body:
* Status — Growing, Reserved (committed to an order), Trade Ready, Retail Ready.

* Track inventory through batches — each batch is a group of plants of the same variety and size moving through the same lifecycle.

* Allow admin to create batches at any time without needing a purchase order from ERP. This is a core daily action on the ERP.

* Allow admin user to add / remove / update quantity of batches.

* Provide a search that lets staff find any plant by latin name and instantly see the full stock picture.

* Every stock change — status updates, quantity adjustments, wastage, new batches — must be recorded with who did it and when. This can be viewed from the batch line and also from a central admin panel which ONLY the super admin can see. This will be a side panel that says 'Updates',…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Staff can create a plant with all required fields. Note: we will be uploading all the data so staff being able to create plants is not very important. Data upload will be manual to start by developers.

* Each plant will have multiple sizes, each with its own SKU (Link to EPOS Now), trade price, retail price, cost price, and dimensions. SKU will be generated automatically — we will also use PLANT ID so we can link to the Label Printer ?

* Staff can create batches manually without a purchase order.

* Batch status changes work correctly and are recorded on plant batch — visible by admin. Also status updates are visible by Super Admin.

* The stock summary for any plant variant correctly shows reserved, growing, trade ready and retail-ready quantities.

* Every stock change has a full aud…

title: Note
body:
A Note is a single timestamped comment authored by a user. The same Note structure is shared across Purchase Orders (PRD-1.1), Quotes, and Orders (both PRD-03). Each parent record holds an array of Notes; admins can append at any time, and existing Notes are immutable (append-only history).

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| author | FK → User | who added the note |
| created\_at | datetime | when the note was added (date \+ time) |
| body | string | the note content |
| parent\_type | enum | Purchase Order / Quote / Order |
| parent\_number | string | po\_number, quote\_number, or order\_number |

**Quote → Order carry-over.** When a Quote converts to an Order, every Note attached to the Quote is re-pointed to the new Order: `parent_type` flips from `Quote` to `Order` a…

title: What Is It
body:
A structured way to order stock from external suppliers, track what's been ordered vs what's actually arrived, and update inventory when shipments come in. Replaces the current process of receiving invoices and manually uploading them.

Admin User will RECONCILE the stock when it arrives. MacPlants staff will note actual quantities which arrived on a piece of paper and this data will be INPUT into the system using the Purchase Order which was created.

title: What It Must Do
body:
* In Settings \> Suppliers. Maintain a supplier database with name, contact, email, phone, and address. (A Supplier can be a customer as well — however these are not linked, separate accounts.)

* One PO equals one supplier — you cannot split a PO across multiple suppliers.

* User is able to easily duplicate a PO — they can then assign the duplicate to a different supplier.

* Staff / Admin User can add Notes to PO at ANY stage on ERP.

* Auto-generate a unique PO reference number for each order.

* Send the PO directly to the supplier by email from within the ERP, with a formatted PDF Doc attached.

* User can create Tasks from PO. User can see all tasks related to PO on the single PO Screen.

* When stock arrives, staff RECONCILE actual quantities received per line. The received quantit…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Staff can CRUD a PO, add line items, add notes, and save as draft.

* Staff can view 'progress' of individual PO — progress stepper at top of screen.

* Once a PO is approved it cannot be updated or deleted. Only cancelled.

* Staff can send a PO to a supplier via email from within the ERP — this will have Supplier Order PDF attached. Staff can download Supplier Order PDF.

* Staff can reconcile stock against individual PO lines with actual quantities, plant size, batch location, batch status and ready date (optional).

* The PO tracking screen shows all open POs with status. User can filter by status. Pills with all the statuses display at top of screen with quantity of POs with that status.

* All PO status changes can be fully audited by system admin through Audit Trail — this is very…

title: PRD-2.0: Customers
body:
The system holds 2 types of customer: trade customers and retail customers.

* Trade customers purchase items through the trade customer portal.

* Retail customers purchase items through the WP website or in the SHOP.

title: What Is It
body:
MacPlants has many different trade customers — landscapers, garden designers, contractors, and garden centres.

The ERP will store an account for each trade customer. Their contact details, addresses, credit terms, payment methods. Trade accounts are created by Macplants staff, not by the customers themselves.

title: Trade Customers — View All
body:
* ERP stores trade customer records: business name, customer type, contact name, email, phone, billing address, delivery address.

* User can search by customer name, business name.

title: Trade Customer Single Page
body:
* Customer detail screen (single page): When an admin clicks on a customer, they see:

  * **Outstanding balance** — sum of unpaid invoice totals.

  * **Available credit** — unallocated credit on account (Issued credit notes minus allocations). Clicking opens the list of contributing credit notes and any allocations already applied (see PRD-3.2).

  * **Current balance** — the net of the two above. Positive \= customer owes MacPlants; negative \= MacPlants holds credit on account.

  * Order / quote / invoice history

* Allow admin to set customer credit limit. This is different for every trade customer so this must be adjustable on trade customer single page.

* Every trade customer has 30 day payment terms

* **Over-credit-limit flow:** When a customer attempts to place an order OVER th…

title: New Trade Customer Flow
body:
* Admin can CRUD a trade customer with full contact details, set credit limit. Admin triggers email which allows user to login to their trade portal account.

* Admin can set per-customer credit terms. Customer CANNOT order over their credit terms. The ERP will have visibility of any active orders which they have in the system at any one time. Orders will be marked as paid MANUALLY by Arien (stage 1).

* When an order is made by a trade customer it will be live in the ERP. Once the order is complete the customer will be issued an invoice via Xero which they can pay by Stripe or Bank Transfer. The order will then be marked as PAID by finance manually by Finance.

* If Customer has reached their Credit Limit they are able to pay for the order instantly using Stripe on the ERP Trade Portal.…

*   
  MacPlants has many plant enthusiasts who buy plants from them.

* Customers can buy online through the WordPress site. When they make a purchase an account will be created automatically on the ERP when they complete the purcase. Look at Echo3 checkout process to see an example of what this is like to move to a subdomain on checkout.

* The customer will checkout on the ERP.

* On their customer account page they will be able to view all previous orders.

title: Deposits
body:
Deposits do NOT get posted to Xero until the full order is processed, as confirmed by Arian. 

A deposit attaches to the **Order**, not to any Invoice. The two are independent:

* **Order ← Deposit.** A deposit is recorded as three fields on the Order (`deposit\_amount`, `deposit\_requested\_at`, `deposit\_received\_at`) plus a customer-facing Deposit Request document with a Stripe payment link. There is no "Deposit" entity, no deposit row in the Invoice table, and no Xero invoice raised for the deposit itself.  
* **Order ← Invoice.** The Invoice flow is unchanged. A single full-amount Invoice is raised at dispatch and posts to Xero per the existing Xero Posting Flow.  
* **Where the two meet.** Only at dispatch. The deposit cash sits in a Xero "Other Debtors" holding account from receipt…

title: Quote → Order
body:
When a QUOTE is raised it is given a number — QUO-1234. We store only the number in the DB. This number stays the same throughout the entire ORDER LIFECYCLE. Prefixes change per stage.: QU-1234 (quote) → ORD-1234 (order).

**Quote Phase**

* **Draft** — created but not sent to the customer

* **Sent** — customer been sent quote, we're waiting on their decision

* **Pending** — customer accepted but not all stock is available yet

* **Approved** — all stock available, awaiting payment from customer to proceed.

* **Rejected** — customer declined (terminal, end of the road)

* **Expired** — Quotes expires after 2 weeks automatically \- REQUIRED

* **Archived** \-

title: Order Phase
body:
Multiple Invoices can be attached to an Order. As the order may change.

* **Pending:**\+ Plants have gone out of stock since the quote was made.

* **Confirmed** \- Customer has PAID or Credit Terms agreed. Order is live, customer is sent order confirmation.  When an order is confirmed, the stock is reserved

* **Ready to Pick** — Order can be easily move to ready to pick from the Confirmed Orders screen. Then when on the Ready to Pick screen, admin can easily assign entire Order to be picked by certain staff member.

* **Trade vs retail pickers:** Macplants operates with separate trade and retail pickers (e.g. Vicky picks trade, Lindsey picks retail). Admin should be easily able to add a picker to an order from Ready to Pick filter screen.

* **Order dashboard / bird's-eye view:** Admin…

title: Invoice Phase
body:
Multiple Invoices can be attached to 1 Order A deposit can be added to an order

If a client orders plants on credit, an invoice is generated when the order is created in the ERP. This invoice is **not sent to Xero at this stage** (see *Xero Posting Flow* below). It is the responsibility of Finance to mark the invoice as **Paid** in the ERP once payment is received \- this is manual and will be done by Finance team.

#### **One Active Invoice per Order**

An order may have multiple invoices over its lifetime, but **only one invoice is active at any given time**:

* The **most recently generated invoice** is considered the *active* invoice.

* All previous invoices are retained for audit purposes and marked as `Superseded`.

   **Invoice Supersession (Pre-Xero)**

If an order is edited befo…

title: Credit Notes
body: Refunded / Credit Noted — see PRD-3.3 below. Any order can be refunded (retail) or credit noted (trade) at any stage, including after completion.

title: Notes & Audit Trail
body:
* Notes are an array of Note records (see Note entity in PRD-1.1) — each note captures the author, the date and time it was added, and the note body. Notes are append-only.

* Quotes and Orders carry **two** note arrays:

  * `external_notes` — visible to the trade customer; either Admin / Super Admin or the customer can append. Used as a back-and-forth communication thread (e.g. substitutions, lead-time questions, delivery scheduling).  
  * `internal_notes` — admin / staff only; never shown to the customer.  
* Admin / Super Admin can add to either array on a Quote or Order at any stage of its lifecycle. Trade customers can only append to `external_notes`.

* Purchase Orders carry a single `notes` array; there is no customer counterpart, so no internal/external split is needed.

* On Quo…

title: Order Lifecycle \- What Is It
body:
The engine that runs the entire order lifecycle — from initial quote through to final delivery and invoicing.

Trade customers can request a quote & then buy plants

Trade customers can see LIVE AVAILABILITY of all the plants which are in stock and 'Trade Ready' from the portal, they can BUY INSTANTLY. Very important to move the friction of them wanting to purchase, should be as easy as possible, too easy even.

Retail orders go straight to the Order Phase. The customer adds this to their desired items in the cart on the WP site. The checkout page takes them to the ERP. They see their basket and are prompted to pay.

title: Quotes \- Single Quote Screen
body:
* Admin must be able to CRUD for quotes \+ Download PDF on single quote screen

* CRUD for line items on Quotes.

* When adding line items to a quote: Admin user types in plant name — it searches through all plant names and allows admin to select a plant name from the database. User can select pot size & quantity. Admin can see in stock quantity of that pot size.

* When user selects pot size the cost price of this plant at this pot size, will automatically autofill the price input field with the COST price; user can override the price field \- there will be a small piece of text to say that this is the COST PRICE.

* **Per-line notes on quotes & orders:** Every line item on a quote or order has its own optional note field. Notes appear directly below the relevant line on the generated PDF…

title: Orders
body:
#### ***Single Order Screen***

* A quote can be converted to an Order once it has been paid (order will be marked as PAID then) OR the customer has made an order within their Credit Terms (marked as unpaid)

* Orders CAN be edited (line items, quantities) while the invoice is still on the ERP and has NOT been posted to Xero. This is important because plants die, customers change their minds and we need to handle that without messy Xero corrections. The admin user can generate a new invoice at any moment with the updated line items. A record of all invoices for that order are kept.

* On single Order page in ERP — admin can see Order Progress Stepper along the top with different stages complete (Ready to Pick, Staged, Packed, Dispatched & Complete).

* **Order title (custom, applies to eve…

title: General Order Logic
body:
An Order is posted to ONLY Xero when it has been dispatched, at this point the order becomes READ-ONLY on the ERP. Any correction after that point must go through the credit note flow (PRD-3.3).

Every order starts as a Quote. Quotes go through: Draft → Sent → (Pending (stock gap)) → Approved / Expired.

An approved quote converts to an Order. An order can either be paid upfront or be within the customer's credit terms./

All Quotes & Orders display cost price of each Unit, the subtotal, VAT Total & Total at the very bottom.

If the customer exceeds their credit limit, the system presents a Stripe payment screen so they can pay immediately rather than being blocked.

Stock is only reserved when the Order is made (payment received or credit terms confirmed). Not before.

If stock dies/sells…

title: Xero Posting Flow
body:
**Previous assumption:** An invoice gets sent to Xero as soon as an order is created.

**New flow** (to avoid messy edits/deletions in Xero when orders change):

* Quote accepted → Order create on ERP & Invoice will be generated automatically on ERP. NOT sent to Xero.

* Customer is sent an email with the PDF attachment of the Invoice.

* Order can be freely edited on the ERP during its lifecycle (adjust quantities, swap lines, add notes). The invoice updates automatically.

* Once the order is Dispatched / Collected, the final invoice is posted to Xero when the order is marked as ‘Complete’

* Once posted to Xero, the invoice becomes read-only on the ERP. Any correction must go through the credit note flow.

*   
* Payment reconciliation in Xero is by bank feed — Ariane matches payments t…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Full Quote → Order lifecycle works with correct status transitions.

* Stock is reserved at Order Confirmation (Customer Pays in full / credit limit / deposit)

* Customer-facing emails are sent with PDF of invoice

* Order status history provides a full audit trail.

* Pending → Approved workflow notifies the manager when stock becomes available.

* Orders can be edited while invoice is on ERP; invoice posts to Xero 24h after dispatch/collection; post-Xero edits go through credit note flow.

* Billing history (invoices \+ payment status \+ balance) is visible on every order.

title: Data Model
body: *Delivery method fields live on the Order itself — there is no separate persisted entity. Listed here to make the delivery-related columns of the Order schema explicit.*

title: Order — delivery-related fields
body:
| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| delivery\_method | enum | Collection / DPD / Pallet / Dutch Trolley / MacPlants Van |
| delivery\_price | decimal | auto for retail DPD; manual for Pallet / Dutch Trolley / Van; £0 for Collection |
| delivery\_date | date | collection / carrier pickup date for non-DPD methods |
| carrier\_reference | string (nullable) | DPD label number etc. |
| number\_of\_boxes | integer (nullable) | captured at pack stage for multi-box orders |

title: What Is It
body: The system that defines how orders get to the customer. Macplants supports 5 delivery methods, each with different rules, capacity limits, and scheduling. When an order is confirmed, the system suggests the best option.

title: What It Must Do
body:
Support five fulfilment methods:

* **Collection** — Free. Available for both retail (via website) and trade. Retail customers CAN order online for collection and should be able to pay online at checkout.

* **DPD** — All retail website orders will use DPD as courier or Collection. (automatic pricing — use current website logic). Trade customers can use DPD as well, admin will enter in how much this will cost.

* **Pallet** — Add manually to invoice (price \+ set date for collection by carrier).

* **Dutch Trolley** — Add manually to invoice (price \+ set date for collection by Groveport).

* **MacPlants Van** — Add manually to invoice (price based on mileage \+ set date).

Additional rules:

* **Collection:** available any business day. Orders approved before midday can be ready next work…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Staff Members can select delivery method for an order & update it at any stage.

* Orders for delivery on a certain day are visible for ERP Admin.

title: Refund / Credit Note
body:
| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| type | enum | Refund (retail) / Credit Note (trade) |
| order | FK → Order |  |
| invoice | FK → Invoice | the original invoice being reversed |
| amount | decimal |  |
| reason | string |  |
| plants\_returned | boolean | if true, restock task is generated |
| external\_reference | string (nullable) | Stripe refund ID for retail refunds; bank transfer reference for the rare trade cash-back handled outside the ERP |
| created\_by | FK → User |  |
| created\_at | datetime |  |
| pushed\_to\_xero | boolean |  |
| pushed\_to\_xero\_at | datetime (nullable) |  |

title: Credit Note Allocation
body:
*Records the application of a Credit Note balance against a future Invoice. Credit notes only — refunds do not allocate. A trade customer has many; a Credit Note has many.*

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| credit\_note | FK → Refund/Credit Note |  |
| invoice | FK → Invoice | the invoice the credit balance is applied against |
| amount | decimal |  |
| allocated\_by | FK → User |  |
| allocated\_at | datetime |  |

title: What Is It
body: When a customer returns plants, changes their mind after completion, the ERP must be able to issue either a refund (retail) or a credit note (trade).

title: Refund Flow (Retail Customers)
body:
Triggered from any completed retail order via the Refund button on the order screen. Refunds are admin-issued — no separate approval gate.

* **Stripe handles the actual money movement.** Retail orders are paid via Stripe at checkout, so refunds are processed directly in the Stripe interface (outside the ERP). The admin then returns to the ERP, clicks Refund, enters amount \+ reason, and captures the Stripe refund ID in `external_reference`.

* **Xero reconciliation is handled manually by Arian.** When the refund is logged in the ERP, the system auto-creates a Finance task on the order, assigned to Arian (see PRD-04). Arian creates the matching credit note in Xero against the original invoice as part of his bank-feed reconciliation work, then completes the task — which sets `pushed_to_xero…

title: Credit Note Flow (Trade Customers)
body:
Triggered from any completed trade order via the Credit Note button on the order screen. Admin-issued, no separate approval gate.

* **No cash moves at issue.** A credit balance is added to the trade customer's account for future use. The credit note is still an accounting document — it adjusts VAT and the customer's Xero balance — so it must reach Xero.

* **Xero reconciliation is handled manually by Arian.** When the credit note is logged in the ERP, the system auto-creates a Finance task on the order, assigned to Arian (see PRD-04). Arian creates the matching credit note in Xero as part of his reconciliation work, then completes the task — which sets `pushed_to_xero = true` on the credit note record. Same pattern as refunds and invoice payments.

* **Lifecycle states:**

  * **Draft** —…

title: Returned Plants (applies to both flows)
body: If plants are physically returned and still viable, a TO DO task is created for staff to add them back into stock (choose location, batch, status — see PRD-1). If the plants are dead/not viable, this is recorded as shrinkage (see PRD-1 — Shrinkage Record).

title: Audit Trail (applies to both flows)
body: Every refund, credit note, and credit note allocation is audited — who raised it, when, against which order/invoice, for what amount, and the reason. All entries appear in the order's Billing History grouped under their originating invoice.

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* A Refund button appears on every completed retail order; a Credit Note button on every completed trade order. The opposite button does not appear on the other customer type.

* Retail refund flow: admin processes the refund in Stripe, then logs it in the ERP with the Stripe refund ID captured in `external_reference`. The system auto-creates a Finance task on the order for Arian. Arian creates the matching credit note in Xero during reconciliation and completes the task, flipping the refund to Issued. The order and refund records are retained in the ERP indefinitely; the refunded amount is netted off the sale value in dashboard / sales reporting.

* Pre-Xero delete works: an admin can hard-delete a Draft refund (mistakes), which also closes the auto-created Finance task. Issued refunds ca…

title: Task
body:
| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| title | string |  |
| description | string |  |
| status | enum | Open / In Progress / Completed / Cancelled |
| type | enum | Finance / Admin / Staff / Other |
| priority | enum | High / Medium / Low |
| assignee | FK → User |  |
| due\_date | date |  |
| attached\_to | FK (polymorphic, nullable) | Quote / Order / Invoice / PO / Plant Batch — null for standalone tasks |
| source | enum | Manual / Auto-generated (system trigger e.g. batch ready, PO overdue) |
| notes | string |  |
| created\_by | FK → User |  |
| created\_at | datetime |  |
| completed\_by | FK → User (nullable) |  |
| completed\_at | datetime (nullable) |  |

title: What Is It
body:
The operational glue that keeps nothing falling through the cracks. Tasks capture every action that needs a human to do something — from verifying a batch is ready, to reconciling a PO, to responding to a new trade order. Notifications keep both staff and customers informed as orders move through their lifecycle. This replaces the current situation where reminders live on Post-its, or in scattered emails.

Internal Tasks surface on the ERP dashboard and in-app:

* Pending orders that can now be approved (stock has arrived).

* Overdue supplier POs.

* Batch ready-date tasks awaiting verification.

* New trade orders awaiting response.

title: What It Must Do
body:
* Tasks appear in the main ERP navigation as a dedicated area. Admin can create tasks manually and assign them to a specific staff member with a due date and priority.

* The system auto-creates tasks from operational triggers:

  * Batch reaches its ready date → task for staff to physically verify and manually update status (readiness is weather-dependent, so status does NOT change automatically).

  * Stock arrives for a pending order → task for manager to review and confirm.

  * Supplier PO is overdue (past expected delivery date) → task to chase supplier.

  * New trade order awaiting response → task for sales staff to review.

  * PO received on site → task to reconcile quantities and create/adjust batches.

  * Order cancelled → task to return reserved stock to correct location.

*…

title: PRD-05: ERP Portals
body:
This PRD breaks down each login portal — its URL and what the user sees once inside. The role definitions live in PRD-0.1; this PRD is the per-portal counterpart. **Staff members do not log in anywhere** (see PRD-0.1) — they use the PWA only.

MacPlants users: Super Admin, Admin. Customers: Retail, Trade.

title: 5.0 Super Admin Portal
body:
* **Login URL:** [staff.macplants.co.uk/login](http://staff.macplants.co.uk/login)  
* **Who:** Josh only (see PRD-0.1).  
* **What they see:** Everything the Admin portal shows (PRD-5.1), plus the **User Action Log** (below) and the staff-management screen for adding/removing staff PWA accounts.

title: User Action Log
body:
A Super-Admin-only investigation tool to detect deliberate or careless mishandling of stock by staff. Narrow scope — not a general audit trail.

#### **What It Must Do**

* On the main menu, an item called "Stock Updates".  
* Surfaces every stock update made by staff members on their PWA, and every stock update made by admins on the ERP, in the last 1 month by default.

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **timestamp** | **datetime** | **when the action occurred** |
| **user** | **FK → User** | **who performed the action** |
| **action\_type** | **enum** | **quantity\_adjust / status\_change / location\_change / batch\_deleted** |
| **plant\_batch** | **FK → Plant Batch** | **the affected batch** |
| **plant\_variant** | **FK → Plant Variant** | **denormalised for fast fi…

title: 5.1 Admin Portal
body:
* **Login URL:** staff.macplants.co.uk/login  
* **Who:** Jake and office staff (see PRD-0.1).  
* **What they see:** Full ERP minus Super-Admin-only tools. Manage POs (PRD-1.1), customers (PRD-2), quotes/orders (PRD-03), refunds and credit notes (PRD-3.2), and stock (PRD-1).

title: 5.2 Staff Portal
body: Staff members do **not** log into the ERP or any web portal. They use the PWA only for stock updates. See PRD-0.1.

title: 5.3 Retail Customer Portal
body:
* **Login URL:** retail.macplants.co.uk/login  
* **Who:** Retail customers (see PRD-2.1.2). Credentials are created by MacPlants staff — no self-registration.  
* **What they see:** Retail-ready stock for browsing and mail-order purchases, plus their order history.

title: 5.4 Trade Customer Portal
body:
* **Login URL:** trade.macplants.co.uk/login  
* **Integration:** Stripe (for top-ups when a basket exceeds the trade customer's credit limit).  
* **Who:** Trade customers (see PRD-2.1.1). Credentials are created by MacPlants staff — no self-registration.  
* **What they see:** Non-retail-ready stock with quantities and trade pricing, account financial summary (outstanding balance, available credit, current balance), and the quote-or-instant-buy purchase paths described below.

title: What Is It
body: A self-service web portal where trade customers log in, search for plants and pricing, build orders, select delivery methods, advise timelines, and track their order status. This replaces the current process where customers email a wish list, staff manually search GrowMaster, and a spreadsheet is sent back.

title: Trade Customer Portal
body:
* Trade customers log in with credentials created by Macplants staff. No self-registration.

* Customers see their account financial summary at the top of the portal: **Outstanding balance** (unpaid invoices), **Available credit** (unallocated credit notes), and **Current balance** (the net of the two — positive \= owed to MacPlants, negative \= MacPlants holds credit). Clicking expands to a full history of invoices and credit notes, with any allocations already applied. Available credit is offered as an option at checkout when raising a new order (see PRD-3.2).

* Customers can search plants by name.

* All plants are shown on the portal unless marked as "unavailable." Even plants that are currently out of stock are visible — Macplants can always reject the order. Plants marked as unavail…

title: Internal Quote Builder (Admin Side)
body:
* Everything the portal offers, plus full stock visibility on what is Available.

* See cost price of each plant size for MacPlants staff. ESSENTIAL. Automatically calculate the profit margin percentage.

* Override pricing on any line manually.

* Add internal notes (not visible to customer) and customer-visible notes per QUOTE — ESSENTIAL. MacPlants may want to discuss any swaps that happened.

* Send quotes to customers with one click.

* **Substitution workflow (IMPORTANT):** When reviewing a customer's quote request, staff can strike through (DELETE) unavailable lines and add substitution lines at the bottom with a note and unit price. The amended quote is sent back to the customer. The customer can then accept or reject each line individually (including substitutions) and submit. Thi…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Trade customers can log in with ERP-created credentials.

* Portal shows searchable plants with availability and prices.

* Customers can build quotes with line items, quantities, delivery date, and fulfilment method.

* Running totals (subtotal, VAT, total) calculate correctly on the trade portal. Deposit handling is admin-side only (PRD-3.1) and does not appear in portal totals.

* Customer-submitted orders always go to Awaiting Response.

* Admin can review, modify, and send quotes back to customers.

* Customers can approve quotes in the portal — OR individual line items of quote.

* Admin has a dedicated Pending Orders screen showing orders awaiting stock.

* Admin Side — Internal quote builder shows full stock visibility.

title: What Is It
body: The first screen admins see when they log in. A control-tower view for managers and the business owner, showing at a glance how the business is performing today, what operational work is in flight, and what needs attention right now. Designed so the owner can walk in on any given morning and understand the state of Macplants in under 30 seconds.

title: Business Performance (Owner View — Core Figures)
body:
* **Sales revenue** — today, this week, this month, this year. Shown ex VAT. Split by retail vs trade.

* **Orders placed** — count of new orders today, this week, this month. Split by retail vs trade.

* **Average order value** — rolling 30-day figure for retail and trade separately.

* **Plants picked** — count of individual plants picked today and this week (pulled from PWA pick completion data).

* **Orders dispatched** — count and total value of orders dispatched today and this week.

* **Outstanding invoices** — total value of invoices in "Awaiting Payment" status, plus ageing buckets (0–30 days, 31–60, 60+).

* **Approved orders in the pipeline** — total value of orders sitting in Approved/Ready to Pick/Staged status (i.e. revenue committed but not yet dispatched).

* Each figure co…

title: Today's Operations (Manager View)
body:
* **Pending quotes** — quotes awaiting a customer response, with count and oldest-first list.

* **Orders awaiting payment** — invoices raised but not yet paid, with age.

* **Orders waiting for stock (Pending)** — highlighted when stock becomes available so the manager can click Confirm to approve.

* **Orders due ready today** — list of orders that need to be picked/packed today.

* **Orders due for dispatch today** — collections, DPD, pallet, Dutch trolley, van — all due out today.

* **New trade orders awaiting response** — count and list of orders in "Awaiting Response" status.

* **Open tasks** — count of open tasks, split by assignee. Owner can see who is busy and who has capacity.

title: Exceptions & Alerts \- WHAT IS MOST IMPORTANT ??
body:
* **Overdue supplier POs** — POs past their expected delivery date.

* **Account-on-hold activity** — any on-hold customer with recent activity (quote, login, payment attempt).

* **Credit limit breaches** — customers who have attempted to order above their credit limit.

* **Low stock alerts** — NOT required for Stage 1 (deferred), but the widget slot should be reserved in the layout.

* **Overstock alerts** — NOT required for Stage 1 (deferred).

* **Long-lead-time orders approaching delivery** — orders invoiced months ago with delivery dates within the next 30 days, so the team can start preparing.

title: General Behaviour
body:
* Every widget links through to the relevant detail screen (clicking "Orders due ready today" opens a filtered order list).

* Custom Calender

* The dashboard is the landing page after login for admin users. It is not shown to warehouse/nursery staff (they land in the PWA).

* All revenue figures are ex VAT by default, with a toggle to view inc VAT.

* The dashboard works on desktop and tablet. Mobile layout stacks widgets vertically.

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Admin lands on the dashboard after login.

* Sales revenue, order count, average order value, plants picked, orders dispatched, outstanding invoices, and pipeline value all show live data with period comparisons.

* Retail vs trade split is visible on revenue and order-count widgets.

* All operational widgets (pending quotes, awaiting payment, pending stock, due ready today, due dispatch today, new trade orders, open tasks) show live counts and link through to filtered lists.

* Exception widgets (overdue POs, on-hold activity, credit breaches, long-lead orders) surface the right records.

* Pending orders with newly available stock are visibly flagged and can be confirmed directly.

* Every widget is clickable and takes the user to the underlying detail screen.

* Figures refresh witho…

title: PRD-06: EPOS Now Integration
body:
In Stage 1 the shop continues to run on EPOS Now exactly as it does today. The ERP runs alongside it. The two systems are not yet plugged together — but we ensure that **the SKUs match across both systems \-** This will mean that future integrations are possible.

For the first stage plants which are Retail Ready & have the location SHOP will not be available online.

When plants are moved to the SHOP they are not available online. This means we won’t sell the same plant twice

At the end of every day an ADMIN will export all orders, upload these to the ERP which will update the stock. This will be easy to do as SKUs will match.

The code generated will be based on a pattern \- TBD. Look into Industry Standard SKU Pattern.

title: What Is It
body:
The API and integration rules that connect the new WordPress retail website to the ERP. WordPress displays products and handles browsing. The ERP owns all data, pricing, stock, and order processing. Checkout happens on the ERP (subdomain), not on WordPress.

**Note:** The WordPress site is built by Paul/Craig. The ERP API is built by the ERP Team. This document defines the contract between the two so both teams build in parallel.

title: What It Must Do
body:
* The ERP provides API endpoints for WordPress to pull: all products with variants, images, and pricing; stock levels per SKU; new products since a given timestamp.

* WordPress syncs products into Custom Post Types with ACF fields. SKU is the linking key between systems.

* Stock sync is manual. When a new plant is added to the ERP is needs to be MANUALLY added to the WP site. As this wont’ happen often it is the best solution.

* On ERP there is an Available / Unavailable on WebShop Override.

* If the ERP API is down, WordPress does not zero out stock. It retries and logs the failure.

* Checkout is handled on the ERP, hosted on a subdomain (e.g. checkout.macplants.co.uk). When a customer clicks checkout on WordPress, they are redirected to the ERP which validates stock, takes payment v…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Stock sync updates only stock fields without overwriting editorial content.

* New products can be synced on demand.

* Basket validates stock availability against the ERP before checkout.

* Checkout redirects to the ERP-hosted page with basket data.

* Customer registration on WordPress pushes data to the ERP.

* Customer account page shows order history from the ERP.

* SKU serves as the unique linking key.

* Sync is re-runnable without duplicates.

title: Meeting Action Items (April 2026\)
body:
* **Josh to confirm HLS/GrowMaster separation** — Can HLS operate independently with ERP data? Josh spoke to Dura-ID previously. Confirm on Friday. This is a BLOCKER for retiring GrowMaster.

* **Jake to write down unusual use cases** — Document edge-case clients and unusual order scenarios (e.g. Marian Boswell year-long hold, customers ordering 700 plants with no intention of buying all). These need system coverage before build.

* **Speak to packing team about dimensions/volumetrics** — Get ballpark formulas for box quantities by plant size. Needed for future auto-calculation of parcels.

* **GrowMaster data export** — Request all orders back to 2018 (not 2014). All customers. All plant data. GrowMaster/website team to provide. Don't spend dev time on this — it's their job.

* **Full phy…

title: Trade Shows (Stock Reservation & Sales Reconciliation)
body: NB \- Tech Team to Ignore this \- no new functionality here \- this is just to document how this will be handled

title: Data Model
body: *No new objects. Trade shows reuse the existing Order (with custom Title) and Purchase Order (for unsold-stock returns) primitives. The only show-specific behaviour is suppressing customer-facing notifications when the linked customer is the dummy show customer.*

title: What Is It
body:
Trade shows (Scone Palace, Powis, RHS shows, Chelsea, farmers' markets, etc.) are a meaningful part of the Macplants business — Scone alone takes \~4 weeks of one person's time to prepare. The ERP must support reserving stock for a show, generating a pick list, taking the show stock out of online/trade availability, and reconciling sales after the show closes.

There is no bespoke "show" module. Trade shows are handled entirely with two existing primitives — a normal Order (with a custom title) and a normal Purchase Order — used in sequence. This keeps the codebase clean and means show data flows through the same reporting, invoicing, and audit machinery as everything else.

title: What It Must Do
body:
* **Step 1 — Create a titled order to reserve show stock.** Admin creates a normal order, sets the Order Title (PRD-03) to something descriptive (e.g. "Scone 2026"), and adds the plants and quantities being taken to the show as line items. The order can sit against a dummy show customer (or any customer admin chooses to use for shows). Creating this order reserves the stock in the standard way, so it immediately disappears from the trade portal and the WordPress retail site. The pick list for the show is generated from this order exactly like any other order — no separate show pick-list flow.

* **Step 2 — After the show, edit the order to reflect what actually sold.** When the team returns from the show, admin edits the line item quantities on the original order down to the quantities tha…

title: How We Know It's Done
body:
* Admin can create an order with a custom title (e.g. "Scone 2026") that reserves the stock and removes it from the trade portal and website.

* A show pick list downloads as a PDF in the same format as any normal order pick list.

* After the show, admin can edit the order quantities down to actual sold quantities and push the order through the standard invoice flow. Payment is recorded manually with a mixed-tender note (terminal / PayPal / cash).

* Admin can then create a separate PO for the returning unsold stock; reconciling that PO puts the plants back into the correct nursery location.

* Show orders never trigger customer-facing emails.

* For any past show, Macplants can pull the original order, the final invoice, and the return PO and see the full taken / sold / returned picture.…
